analysis_metadata.paper_title: Observation of a New Particle in the Search for the Standard Model Higgs Boson with the ATLAS Detector at the LHC
analysis_metadata.energy: pp collisions at √s = 7 TeV (2011) and √s = 8 TeV (2012)
analysis_metadata.luminosity: 4.8 fb−1 at 7 TeV; 5.8–5.9 fb−1 at 8 TeV
analysis_metadata.notes: Diphoton channel as described in Section 5 of arXiv:1207.7214v2.

analysis_objectives.target_process: SM Higgs boson production with H→γγ
analysis_objectives.description: Search for a narrow resonance in the diphoton invariant-mass spectrum using categorized unbinned likelihood fits to mγγ.
analysis_objectives.special_considerations: Analysis uses ten categories across 7 TeV and 8 TeV datasets; background is modeled from data with category-specific functional forms and a bias-constraint term.

signal_signatures.event_topology_description: Two isolated photon candidates in the electromagnetic calorimeter; events are categorized by photon conversion status, photon η regions, pTt (orthogonal component of diphoton pT), and a VBF-enriched 2-jet topology.
signal_signatures.special_considerations: About 70% of signal events in the 2-jet category come from VBF.

background_processes.special_treatment: Modeled by fitting the mγγ spectrum in each category with an analytic function (category-dependent).

background_processes.process_name: γ+jet with jet misidentified as photon (γj)
background_processes.special_treatment: Included effectively in the fitted background model per category.
background_processes.notes: Mentioned as contributing background in H→γγ channel.

background_processes.process_name: jet+jet with one/two jets misidentified as photons (jj)
background_processes.special_treatment: Included effectively in the fitted background model per category.
background_processes.notes: Mentioned as contributing background in H→γγ channel.

background_processes.special_treatment: Included effectively in the fitted background model per category.

signal_regions.selection_summary: VBF-enriched 2-jet category: require ≥2 jets with |η|<4.5 and pT>25 GeV (pT>30 GeV for jets with 2.5<|η|<4.5 in 8 TeV analysis). For jets with |η|<2.5 require jet vertex fraction (JVF) ≥0.75. Additional VBF-motivated cuts: Δη(j1,j2)>2.8, mjj>400 GeV, and Δφ(γγ system, dijet system)>2.6.
signal_regions.optimization_strategy: Topology-based categorization to enhance VBF sensitivity.
signal_regions.purpose: Enhance sensitivity to VBF production; ~70% of signal in this category from VBF.

signal_regions.selection_summary: Two unconverted photons; both photons central (|η|<0.75); pTt<60 GeV. Baseline photon selection: photons in |η|<2.37 excluding 1.37≤|η|<1.52; leading ET>40 GeV, subleading ET>30 GeV; selected with diphoton trigger.
signal_regions.optimization_strategy: Categorize by conversion/η/pTt to exploit differing mass resolution and S/B.
signal_regions.purpose: High mass resolution and S/B category for resonance search.
signal_regions.special_considerations: Photon ID uses NN-based selection for 7 TeV and cut-based selection for 8 TeV.

signal_regions.selection_summary: Two unconverted photons; both |η|<0.75; pTt≥60 GeV; baseline photon selection as above.
signal_regions.optimization_strategy: High pTt targets VBF/VH/ttH-enriched events with higher pTt.
signal_regions.purpose: Improve sensitivity by separating higher pTt signal-like events.

signal_regions.selection_summary: Two unconverted photons; not both in |η|<0.75 (unconverted rest); pTt<60 GeV; baseline photon selection as above.
Two unconverted photons; unconverted rest; pTt≥60 GeV; baseline photon selection as above.
At least one converted photon; both photons central (|η|<0.75); pTt<60 GeV; baseline photon selection as above.
signal_regions.special_considerations: Converted photons can have one or two tracks matched to the calorimeter cluster.

signal_regions.selection_summary: At least one converted photon; both |η|<0.75; pTt≥60 GeV; baseline photon selection as above.
At least one converted photon; converted rest (all other events not in converted central or converted transition); pTt<60 GeV; baseline photon selection as above.
At least one converted photon; converted rest; pTt≥60 GeV; baseline photon selection as above.
At least one converted photon with 1.3<|η|<1.75 (converted transition category); no pTt split; baseline photon selection as above.
signal_regions.optimization_strategy: Dedicated category for poorer resolution transition-region events.
signal_regions.purpose: Maintain sensitivity while accounting for differing resolution/background in transition η region.

control_regions.selection_summary: Control regions used to cross-check simulated background shapes with data (details not specified).
control_regions.justification: Background shapes in simulation were cross-checked using data from control regions as part of bias studies for background parameterizations.

fit_setup.fit_purpose: Extract signal strength and evaluate excess significance in H→γγ using categorized mγγ spectra.
fit_setup.differences_between_fit_versions: Unbinned likelihood constructed from ten categories for each of the 7 TeV and 8 TeV samples; background parameterizations differ by category.
fit_setup.special_considerations: Signal model per category is Crystal Ball + Gaussian tails; background modeled from data in 100–160 GeV window with bias constraint term treated as systematic.

results.numerical_result: 126.0 ± 0.4 (stat) ± 0.4 (sys) GeV (from high-resolution channels including H→γγ and H→ZZ→4ℓ)
results.dominant_uncertainties: Photon/electron energy scale and resolution systematics contribute to overall mass and significance uncertainties.
results.notes: Mass quoted for the observed new particle in the combined analysis; channel-specific mass not specified in extracted text.

analysis_design_insights.design_feature: Categorization by photon conversion, η region, and pTt
analysis_design_insights.description: Events are split into nine non-2jet categories based on converted/unconverted photons, central/rest/transition η regions, and a pTt split at 60 GeV (except converted transition).
analysis_design_insights.reason_for_use: Improve sensitivity by exploiting differences in mass resolution and signal-to-background, and by enriching VBF/VH/ttH-like kinematics in high-pTt categories.
analysis_design_insights.special_considerations: pTt is defined as the component of the diphoton pT orthogonal to the axis defined by the difference of the two photon momenta.

analysis_design_insights.description: Dedicated 2-jet selection with VBF topology cuts (Δηjj, mjj, Δφ(γγ, jj)).
analysis_design_insights.reason_for_use: Enhance sensitivity to VBF production; ~70% of signal in this category from VBF.
analysis_design_insights.special_considerations: Includes pile-up mitigation via JVF requirement.

analysis_design_insights.design_feature: Data-driven background modeling with bias constraint
analysis_design_insights.description: Background in each category is fit from data (100–160 GeV) with category-specific analytic functions; potential bias is evaluated using simulated backgrounds and incorporated as a systematic via an added signal-like term in the likelihood.
analysis_design_insights.reason_for_use: Control spurious signal from imperfect background functional forms while retaining statistical power.
analysis_design_insights.special_considerations: Preselection requires bias <20% of statistical uncertainty on fitted signal yield.

sensitivity_drivers.dominant_signal_feature: Narrow peak in m_gammagamma with category-dependent mass resolution
sensitivity_drivers.dominant_background_rejection_feature: Photon ID and isolation; categorization exploiting conversion/η and pTt; VBF dijet topology in 2-jet category
sensitivity_drivers.main_limiting_systematics: Photon reconstruction/identification efficiency and photon energy resolution/scale; background-model bias uncertainty
